items.briefing:
Australian organisations should be aware that many cyber incidents stem from shared infrastructure vulnerabilities rather than individual system failures — meaning an attack on a common supplier or platform can affect multiple organisations simultaneously. This is especially relevant for registered training organisations using shared EdTech platforms, as attackers are increasingly targeting software suppliers rather than schools or training providers directly.

Australia's communications regulator ACMA has also flagged new compliance priorities for 2026–27 focused on consumer protection, so ensure your organisation's communication and data practices meet current obligations.

**Tip:** Check that your critical software vendors have their own strong security practices in place — ask them about their incident response plans.

featured.title: Australia's resilience problem isn't redundancy. It's shared infrastructure
featured.quote: Shared infrastructure vulnerabilities could simultaneously knock out supposedly redundant critical systems across Australia.
